executive_summary: The session shows opportunities to refine nonverbal delivery. Focus on tightening gestures, improving audience orientation after board/note checks, and ensuring a more even room scan. These adjustments can enhance student engagement and perceived connection.

priority_actions.title: Tighten the peak size of gestures
priority_actions.why_it_matters: Large bursts of motion can distract from the teaching point when they are not tightly timed.
priority_actions.what_we_saw: 00:00-00:15 had gesture peaks that looked larger than the teaching point required. 00:15-00:30 had gesture peaks that looked larger than the teaching point required.
priority_actions.what_to_try_next: Keep big gestures for true emphasis and use smaller controlled hand movements elsewhere.

priority_actions.title: Turn back toward the audience sooner
priority_actions.why_it_matters: Audience-facing body orientation supports perceived connection and makes eye-contact cues more visible.
priority_actions.what_we_saw: 00:00-00:15 spent longer than needed turned away from the audience after checks. 00:15-00:30 spent longer than needed turned away from the audience after checks.
priority_actions.what_to_try_next: After each board or note check, reset your shoulders and chin back toward the room.

priority_actions.title: Deliberately sweep the room
priority_actions.why_it_matters: More even attention across the room helps students feel included and keeps engagement distributed.
priority_actions.what_we_saw: 00:00-00:15 stayed more fixed on one part of the room than on a left-center-right sweep. 00:15-00:30 stayed more fixed on one part of the room than on a left-center-right sweep.
priority_actions.what_to_try_next: At each major point, pause and sweep left-center-right before moving on.

additional_observation_inventory.title: Open the stance between points
additional_observation_inventory.evidence: 00:00-00:15 showed a more guarded arm-and-shoulder position between teaching points. 00:15-00:30 showed a more guarded arm-and-shoulder position between teaching points.
additional_observation_inventory.suggested_response: Check whether transitions keep the elbows and shoulders open instead of folding inward.

low_confidence_watchlist.title: Open the stance between points
low_confidence_watchlist.why_watch: The signal is visible, but it is not yet sustained enough to justify real corrective feedback.
low_confidence_watchlist.what_we_saw: 00:00-00:15 showed a more guarded arm-and-shoulder position between teaching points. 00:15-00:30 showed a more guarded arm-and-shoulder position between teaching points.
low_confidence_watchlist.what_to_monitor_next: Check whether transitions keep the elbows and shoulders open instead of folding inward.

watch_for: gesture size matching the importance of the point
quick shoulder-and-chin resets back to the room after board/note checks
visible sweeps of attention across the room
open elbows and shoulders during transitions
confidence_notes: Face visibility is limited in parts of the clip, so eye-contact and facial-tone claims are less certain.
Hand visibility drops in parts of the clip, so gesture labels are less certain.
Face coverage dropped below 95%; audience orientation and facial scores are less stable.
Hand coverage dropped below 85%; gesture classification is less stable.
